title: kNN queries in Virtuoso [Problematic Geometry Evaluation]
description:
Hi,
I have loaded in Virtuoso two RDF triple datasets A & B. Both datasets have spatial and non-spatial triples. Regarding spatial triples, A includes POINT, LINESTRING and POLYGON geometies, while B has POINT and MULTIPOINT geometries.
I also have some kNN queries, divided in two classes. The first class C1: points, the second class C2: non-points. The C1 queries ask for the k nearest spatial entities that have a POINT geometry. The C2 queries ask for the k nearest spatial entities that have a non POINT geometry (e.g., LINESTRING, POLYGON in A, MULTIPOINT in B).
I implemented the kNN queries as the last example in link:
http://docs.openlinksw.com/virtuoso/rdfsparqlgeospat/
where, instead of DESC, I have ASC, and I have a LIMIT k.
During kNN evaluation on A & B in Virtuoso, I have encountered two problems:

For C1 kNN queries (points), the kNN results are not in the right order, and also several of them are missing from the right kNN solution. This happens for A dataset, since on B I execute only non-point kNN queries.
For C2 kNN queries (non-points), on A I got a zero result, while on B I got the error: "Function st_distance() expects a geometry of type 1 as argument 0, not geometry of type 4"

Since Virtuoso supports geometries, as you say in link:
http://docs.openlinksw.com/virtuoso/sqlrefgeospatial7enchance/
and I can load geometry triples without problems, it seems that bif:st_distance function does not work right and generates the (1) & (2) problems mentioned above.
I am waiting a formal answer for the (1) & (2) problems, since I will provide the kNN evaluation in Virtuoso to a research paper targeted to a very significant conference.
Best,
KT.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE spatial_entities (id INT PRIMARY KEY, geometry GEOMETRY NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO spatial_entities (id, geometry, name) VALUES (1, ST_Point(-2.5, 52.5), 'Entity A');
INSERT INTO spatial_entities (id, geometry, name) VALUES (2, ST_LineString(ST_Point(-3, 53), ST_Point(-2, 54)), 'Entity B');

SELECT DISTINCT s, ST_Distance(g, ST_Point(-2.5, 52.5)) AS distance FROM spatial_entities WHERE ST_Intersects(geometry, ST_Point(-2.5, 52.5)) AND ST_Distance(g, ST_Point(-2.5, 52.5)) < 20 ORDER BY distance ASC LIMIT 5;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM spatial_entities WHERE ST_Intersects(geometry, ST_PolygonFromText('POLYGON((-3 52, -1 52, -1 54, -3 54, -3 52))'));
key_bug_features: kNN queries return incorrect results for spatial entities
